focus: Hallmark default build and redesign behavior versus shipped interface and foundations contracts
noveltyJustification: Eight rows establish new redesign, route, scope, and structural-catalog decisions; three refine prior overlap into exact surgical targets.
keyQuestions: Does shipped sk-design already have a redesign equivalent?
Is a separate redesign or variant command justified?
Which Hallmark structural heuristics and catalogs merit surgical reuse?
answeredQuestions: Shipped design-interface and /interface:design already provide redesign and multi-direction equivalents.
No separate redesign or variant command is justified.
Multi-surface coherence and generation-time structural option framing are the highest-value surgical additions.
ruledOut: A separate redesign mode or command
Copying the 21 macrostructures or 50 component archetypes as presets
Replacing foundations responsive guidance with fixed viewport recipes

finding: Hallmark redesign is a preservation/scope branch over the default design engine, not an independent design methodology.
A separate redesign command duplicates the shipped redesign task lane and conditional intake.
A separate variant command duplicates directions, variation-diversity, and private variation procedures.
Add single-surface versus multi-surface classification and shared-system coherence invariants.
Require a named whole-page composition and section order before component styling.
Add independently worded structural completeness prompts without copying Hallmark's six-axis table.
Prefer whole-page composition as the diversity axis when structural sameness is the observed failure.
Distinguish whole-page composition from section-level component treatment and commit the former first.
Existing anti-repetition and mobile-collapse checks already cover Hallmark's relevant post-plan gates.
Require each accepted structural option to declare its narrow-context transformation and preserved hierarchy.
Foundations should validate and systematize accepted structure, not expose a structural chooser or variant lane.

reason: Existing interface redesign lane and intake already own the behavior.
Existing directions lane and private procedures already own the behavior.
Fixed catalogs conflict with subject grounding and no-reusable-preset guardrails; substantial copying also requires MIT notice.
Foundations' viewport/input/capability/posture adaptation model is broader and more durable.
